item.title: Trump’s latest AI czar has already resigned
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: The director role for the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) has become a revolving door since David Sacks left his position as czar.

item.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.

item.title: AI’s most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Under the new system, the protocol will take a looser, "stateless" approach to session IDs on the server side, similar to how most ordinary websites already work.

item.title: X relaunches a rebuilt Android app after year-long effort
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: X says the rebuilt version of its Android app is now available globally.

item.title: OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Talk of banning Chinese-made open-weight LLMs reveals the challenge of turning AI into a business.

item.title: Natural raises $30M to reinvent payments for AI agents — and take on Stripe
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The one-year-old startup aims to reinvent financial architecture for autonomous AI transactions.

item.title: Judge pauses $110B Paramount-Warner Bros. merger
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The lawsuit from the states alleges that the deal would harm movie theaters, basic cable distributors, and audiences.

item.title: Adobe camera app’s new feature will critique your photos using AI
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Adobe's Project Indigo can now remove all kinds of backgrounds from photos you snap using the app.

item.title: Hackers are exploiting recently patched WordPress bugs, putting millions of websites at risk
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Two critical security flaws in WordPress’ software have given hackers the chance to remotely take over tens of millions of websites, according to an estimate by a cybersecurity researcher.

item.title: YouTube clarifies policies around AI slop and upsetting videos
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: YouTube has updated its monetization policies to more clearly define the kinds of AI-generated and low-quality videos that can’t earn ad revenue.

item.title: Inference startup Infinity raises $15M from Touring Capital, OpenAI and Anthropic researchers
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: AI infrastructure company Infinity announced Monday a $15 million raise at a $100 million valuation from investors including Touring Capital, Principal VC, and researchers from companies such as OpenAI and Anthropic.

item.title: Hackers stole ‘significant’ amount of data from tech firm relied on by thousands of US hospitals and pharmacies
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Edinburgh-based tech firm Craneware said customer data was stolen during a cyberattack. The company makes software that thousands of U.S. hospitals, pharmacies, and clinics rely on for billing patients, potentially exposing health data.

item.title: Watch Flock Safety CEO Garrett Langley discuss the future of surveillance at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Flock Safety sits right at the center of the debate over where the line should be drawn between privacy and public safety. That’s why we’re bringing Flock’s founder and CEO Garrett Langley to the stage to speak about those very issues.

item.title: Hugging Face confirms breach affected internal datasets and credentials, urges users to take action
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Hugging Face is urging users to rotate any access tokens stored on the platform and review account activity.

item.title: StrictlyVC returns to New York City September 10 to celebrate a huge year for the city’s startup community
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: For the first time since 2024, StrictlyVC is coming back to New York City — and we're bringing the kind of access you’d expect from an under-wraps event to the whole startup, VC, and dealmaking community.

item.title: Netflix paid $587M for Ben Affleck’s AI filmmaking startup
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Netflix revealed that it paid $587 million in cash for InterPositive, a startup co-founded by Ben Affleck.

item.title: What to watch for after Jensen Huang’s Japan visit
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: Jensen Huang left Tokyo with deals spanning Japan's entire tech ecosystem.

item.title: Can an Apple lawsuit derail OpenAI’s hardware plans?
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether Apple's lawsuit will cast a shadow over OpenAi's much-discussed plans to get into hardware and go public.

item.title: TechCrunch Mobility: The battle over robotaxi rules
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

item.title: ‘Odyssey’ director Christopher Nolan calls AI an obvious ‘Trojan horse’
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: "Everybody knows the Greeks are inside."
